items.q: What is the difference between relief and intaglio printing?
items.correct: Relief prints from raised surfaces; intaglio prints from incised lines below the surface.
items.misconception: They are different names for the same process.
items.random_wrong: Relief is for paper, intaglio is for fabric.
items.why: Students don't grasp the inverse logic. Lino cuts AWAY non-printing areas (relief - raised inked); etching incises lines that HOLD ink below the surface (intaglio - ink pressed up into paper).

items.correct: The unconscious mind, dreams, and Freudian psychology.
items.why: Students dismiss Surrealism as 'weird'. It was a serious avant-garde movement informed by psychoanalysis, using techniques like automatism to access the unconscious.

items.q: What were Impressionist painters primarily trying to capture?
items.correct: The fleeting effects of light and atmosphere at a particular moment.
items.misconception: Their feelings or first impressions of a scene.
items.why: Students take 'impression' as personal feeling. Impressionism was about visual perception - how light actually appears on surfaces, painted quickly outdoors before light changed.

items.q: Which artist's infinity nets typify obsessive mark-making?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Yayoi Kusama. “Cy Twombly” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many screens does an HL Process Portfolio maximum allow?
items.why: The real figure is 18 screens. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
